titleStmt.title: SII 26.266: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 266. (A.R. No. 251 of 1909.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Varadarāja-perumāḷ temple.

p.hi: Vīrapratāpa-Dēvarāya-mahārāya I: Ś. 131[8] for 1338, Durmukhi, Pushya-ba. 13, Friday = 1417 A.D., January 15.

summary.p: This inscription records an order of the king exempting the maṉṟāḍis of a number of nāḍus like Tagaḍa-nāḍu, Eyyil-nāḍu, and Puṟamalaināḍu and several paṟṟus like Kaliṅgaṟai-paṟṟu, Vārūrp-paṟṟu, Mukkaṉūrppaṟṟu, Ārppūrp-paṟṟu, Paiyūrp-paṟṟu, Kuḍivūrp-paṟṟu and Tenśīrmaṅgalappaṟṟu in Muḷvāyrājya from the payment of the tax called puṟakūli, for grazing their sheep, goats and cattle in the forests of the above-mentioned places, because, the māṉṟāḍis had been paying a certain tax called taḷaiviṟai to the king.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . rājādhirāja parameśvaran śrīvīrapratāpa devarāya mahārāya (rā)yar pradhivirājyam paṇṇi Aruḷāniṉṟa śakābdam 1000 3 100 10 8 Itaṉ mel cellāniṉṟa dummukhi samvatsarattu puṣya bahuḷatrayodaśiyum śukravārattu nāḷ śrīmatukāvaṉṉatāta Uṭaiyar muḷvāya I
rācciyattukkuc cellum takaṭanāṭu Eyyilnāṭu kaliṅkaṟaippaṟṟu vārurppaṟṟu mukkaṉūrppaṟṟup puṟamalaināṭu Ārppūrppaṟṟu paiyūrppaṟṟu tā ppaṟṟu patu koṅkāṭa ṭṭai kuṭivūrppaṟṟum tencirmaṅkalappaṟṟu cantirapa ppaṟṟu Uḷḷiṭṭa pala maṉṟāṭikaḷukkuk kuṭutta Olai nam tāṅkaḷ paṭaitta Āṭu māṭu
Intakkāṭukaḷ meyntatukku Araimaṉaikkut taḷaiviṟai Iṟukkayiṉāle tāṅkaḷ Iṉṉāṭukaḷil Āṭumāṭukaḷ meyntatukku puṟ kūlikku paṉṉi ṉṟu taṅkaḷaik koḷḷappaṭaialla Inta nāṭṭilavarkaḷum taṅkaḷaippuṟkūli tarakkaṭavatu Eṉṟu kuṭutta śāsanam Inta śāsanattu Iyātu Oruvarahitam paṇṇiṉavaṉ keṅkaikkaraiyil kurāl pacuvaik koṉṟāṉ pāpattile pukakkaṭavaṉākavum Aṁ na dā ta na ba ra ha This expression means that the writing is of Annadātā. It is engraved in Kannaḍa characters.
Digital edition of SII 26.266 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
